title: Quiz: Molecular Hamiltonians
description: Test your understanding of molecular Hamiltonians and fermion-to-qubit mappings

questions.question: What do creation and annihilation operators represent in second quantization?

options.text: Adding and removing energy from the system
Adding and removing electrons from orbitals

questions.explanation: Creation operators (a†) add an electron to an orbital, while annihilation operators (a) remove an electron. This formalism naturally handles the indistinguishability of electrons.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Z string in Jordan-Wigner transformation?

options.text: To reduce the number of qubits

questions.explanation: The Z string (Z⊗Z⊗...⊗Z on all preceding qubits) ensures that the mapped operators satisfy the fermionic anticommutation relations {aᵢ, a†ⱼ} = δᵢⱼ.

questions.question: How does the Pauli weight scale with system size in Jordan-Wigner?
questions.explanation: In Jordan-Wigner, the Z string grows linearly with the orbital index, resulting in O(n) Pauli weight (non-identity operators) for operators acting on orbital n.

questions.question: The Bravyi-Kitaev transformation always requires fewer qubits than Jordan-Wigner.
questions.explanation: Both Jordan-Wigner and Bravyi-Kitaev use the same number of qubits (one per spin orbital). Bravyi-Kitaev's advantage is reduced Pauli weight O(log n) versus O(n), not fewer qubits.

questions.question: How many qubits are needed for a molecule with N spatial orbitals?
questions.explanation: Each spatial orbital has two spin orbitals (alpha and beta), and each spin orbital is mapped to one qubit. Thus, N spatial orbitals require 2N qubits.

questions.question: How does the number of terms in a molecular Hamiltonian scale with the number of orbitals N?
questions.explanation: The two-body integrals h_pqrs have four indices, leading to O(N⁴) terms in the worst case. Symmetry can reduce this, but the scaling remains polynomial.

questions.question: What symmetry can be used to reduce the number of qubits for H₂?
questions.explanation: H₂ has 2 electrons with total spin zero. Using particle number conservation and spin symmetry (Sz conservation), we can reduce the Hilbert space dimension significantly.

questions.question: Which are one-body terms in the molecular Hamiltonian? (Select all)
questions.explanation: One-body terms involve single electrons: kinetic energy (a) and electron-nuclear attraction (b). Electron-electron repulsion (c) is a two-body term. Nuclear repulsion (d) is a constant added separately.
